# Phase 03: Safety Validation Layer — Verification Report

**Phase Goal:** Every story generated by the app passes safety validation before reaching a parent. The system uses Claude Haiku to validate each story, retries with a reinforced prompt if flagged, and returns a friendly error after 3 failed attempts.
**Verified:** 2026-03-25T09:07:30Z
**Status:** PASSED
**Re-verification:** No — initial verification

---

## Goal Achievement

### Observable Truths

| #  | Truth                                                                                   | Status     | Evidence                                                                            |
|----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1  | parseValidationResponse returns safe:true for "SAFE" (case-insensitive, whitespace)     | VERIFIED   | safety.ts lines 25-26; 3 test cases passing                                         |
| 2  | parseValidationResponse returns safe:false with reason for "UNSAFE: ..." input          | VERIFIED   | safety.ts lines 27-30; 2 test cases covering mixed-case and with/without reason      |
| 3  | parseValidationResponse returns safe:false for unparseable input (fail closed)          | VERIFIED   | safety.ts line 31 returns "Unparseable validation response"; 2 test cases passing    |
| 4  | generateSafeStory returns ok:true with story when first attempt passes validation       | VERIFIED   | safety.ts lines 59-83; test verifies 2 mockCreate calls (1 generate + 1 validate)   |
| 5  | generateSafeStory retries up to 2 times using reinforced prompt on retries              | VERIFIED   | safety.ts lines 59-63 (attempt > 0 uses buildReinforcedSystemPrompt); test confirmed |
| 6  | generateSafeStory returns ok:false reason "all_attempts_failed" after 3 failed attempts | VERIFIED   | safety.ts line 84; test verifies 6 mockCreate calls and ok:false result              |
| 7  | POST /api/generate returns complete story as plain text (not streamed)                  | VERIFIED   | route.ts line 55-57: `new Response(result.story, {Content-Type: text/plain})`       |
| 8  | The returned story has been validated by Haiku before reaching the client               | VERIFIED   | route.ts line 52 calls generateSafeStory which always runs validateStory internally  |
| 9  | System retries silently up to 2 times with reinforced prompt if flagged                 | VERIFIED   | Delegated entirely to generateSafeStory — no retry logic visible at route layer      |
| 10 | All 3 attempts failed: client receives JSON error with friendly message and status 500  | VERIFIED   | route.ts lines 61-66 and 69-74: exact D-07 message, status 500                      |
| 11 | Client never sees "unsafe", "validation", or "retry" in any 500 response string        | VERIFIED   | route.ts error strings confirmed clean; only input 400 uses "validationError" var    |

**Score:** 11/11 truths verified

### Requirements Coverage

| Requirement | Source Plan | Description                                                                                                                                                | Status    | Evidence                                                                                    |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| SAFE-01     | 03-01, 03-02 | Every generated story passes multi-layer content filtering: prompt-level safety constraints baked into system prompt, plus separate post-generation validation call using lightweight model | SATISFIED | buildValidationPrompt in prompts.ts; validateStory using Haiku in safety.ts; route delegates to generateSafeStory |
| SAFE-02     | 03-01, 03-02 | If validation flags a story as uncertain or unsafe, app silently retries with modified prompt reinforcing safety constraints (up to 2 retries)             | SATISFIED | generateSafeStory loop (MAX_ATTEMPTS=3); attempt > 0 uses buildReinforcedSystemPrompt; retry is invisible to client |
| SAFE-03     | 03-01, 03-02 | If a safe story cannot be produced after all retries, app displays graceful friendly error — unsafe story never shown to parent                            | SATISFIED | route.ts returns `"We weren't able to create a story right now. Please try again."` with status 500 when result.ok===false |

No orphaned requirements. All three SAFE requirement IDs are claimed by both Plan 01 and Plan 02 and are fully satisfied.

### Human Verification Required

### 1. Live Story Generation — Full End-to-End

**Test:** Start the dev server (`npm run dev`). POST to `/api/generate` with `{"name":"Luna","age":4,"theme":"Dreams & Clouds","duration":5}`. Confirm the response arrives as a single complete text payload (not chunked/streamed), contains Luna's name, and reads as age-appropriate content.
**Expected:** `Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8`, complete story text in one response, no safety-related terminology visible.
**Why human:** Cannot invoke a live Anthropic API call programmatically in this verification context. This was already approved during Plan 02 Task 2 execution on 2026-03-25.

---

### Gaps Summary

No gaps. All automated checks passed. The phase goal is fully achieved:

- The safety library (`safety.ts`) is substantive, wired, and data-flowing — not a stub.
- All 23 unit tests pass, covering all edge cases documented in the plan behavior block.
- The route handler is genuinely thin: it delegates all generation and validation to `generateSafeStory`, which enforces the 3-attempt retry loop with reinforced prompts.
- No streaming artifacts remain. The response is buffered plain text.
- The friendly D-07 error message is exact and used in both error branches (result.ok===false and unexpected catch).
- All three SAFE requirements are satisfied by the implementation.
